config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not routinely override individual rights, because that is how abuse and unfairness start. A society is healthier when it protects personal freedom, dignity, and due process, even when that is inconvenient for the majority. From my perspective, the best approach is to balance both, with individual rights as the baseline that society must justify limiting.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan daripada hak individu secara umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami penuh dengan contoh bahwa ketika mayoritas atau negara diberi terlalu banyak kuasa, hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil mudah disalahgunakan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi pembatasan hak individu harus sangat sempit, jelas alasannya, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan aturan default. Bagi saya, hak individu adalah dasar; masyarakat harus membuktikan mengapa pembatasan itu benar-benar perlu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that logic is too easy to abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to “common good” can be used to silence dissent, protect power, or pressure people to conform, so I am wary of giving the collective a blank check. At the same time, I do not believe rights are absolute in every situation, because public safety, disaster response, and social order sometimes justify limited restrictions. The key difference is that any limit on individual rights must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not treated as the default rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika kami punya banyak pelajaran bahwa ketika “kepentingan masyarakat” dijadikan alasan utama, hak minoritas dan kebebasan sipil sering jadi korban. Saya paham bahwa keamanan publik, bencana, dan ketertiban bisa menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus luar biasa sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan diberi cek kosong. Argumen bahwa masyarakat butuh prioritas memang terdengar masuk akal, namun tanpa batas yang kuat, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, hak individu tetap menjadi titik awal, dan masyarakat hanya boleh membatasi hak itu jika benar-benar perlu dan dapat dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the claim that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual, because in practice that often becomes a shortcut for coercion and abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, I am especially wary of vague appeals to the "common good," since they can be used to pressure dissenters, excuse corruption, or make minorities pay the price for decisions they did not choose. Public safety and disaster response can justify limited restrictions, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable rather than treated as the default. For me, individual rights are the baseline, and society has to earn any exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan minoritas dan membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Memang ada situasi seperti keselamatan publik, bencana, atau ketertiban yang bisa menuntut pembatasan hak, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan aturan default. Kalau hak individu tidak ditempatkan sebagai titik awal, masyarakat terlalu mudah mengorbankan kebebasan demi kenyamanan mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat harus membuktikan bahwa pembatasan benar-benar perlu, bukan sebaliknya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
